body:
## Summary

Fixes [CAMEL-23361](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CAMEL-23361).

The `camel-aws2-s3` producer did not expose the HTTP status code returned by S3, leaving `Exchange.HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE` (`CamelHttpResponseCode`) unset on every operation. Routes wanting to react to the HTTP status (e.g. distinguish a `204 No Content` from a `200 OK` after a delete or put) had no way to read it.

## Changes

- Added a `populateHttpResponseCode(AwsResponse, Message)` helper in `AWS2S3Producer` and `AWS2S3StreamUploadProducer` that reads `sdkHttpResponse().statusCode()` and sets it on `Exchange.HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE`.
- Invoked the helper from every producer operation (PUT, COPY, DELETE, GET, LIST, HEAD, ACL, tagging, versioning, policy, multipart upload, single op, stream upload, etc.).
- Added `AWS2S3ProducerHttpResponseCodeTest` with 6 tests using a mocked `S3Client` to verify the header is populated for `deleteObject`, `deleteBucket`, `createBucket`, `listObjects`, `listBuckets`, and `deleteBucketPolicy`.

## Test plan

- [x] `mvn -DskipITs test` (32 tests, all green)
- [x] `mvn -DskipTests -Psourcecheck install` succeeds
- [ ] Existing localstack-based ITs continue to pass on CI

_Claude Code on behalf of Andrea Cosentino_
